title: schizaea dichotoma
intro: Schizaea dichotoma, also known as the Forked Staghorn Fern or Schizaea, is a species of fern native to Australia and New Zealand, characterized by its unique forked fronds that resemble deer antlers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Schizaea dichotoma are attributed to its antioxidant and antiseptic properties, which can help protect against infections and promote wound healing. The plant can be prepared as a tea or tincture for its medicinal uses, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for therapeutic purposes.
